items.briefing:
A recent study found most organisations have already experienced an AI-related security incident, highlighting growing risks as AI tools become more common in workplaces. For Australian staff, this is a timely reminder that using AI platforms carelessly — such as entering sensitive work data into unvetted tools — can expose your organisation to breaches. On a positive note, a Dark Reading report suggests Australians face a reduced overall cybercrime risk compared to previous years, though pressure is shifting toward small and medium businesses, which often have fewer protections in place.

**Practical tip:** Before using any AI tool for work tasks, check with your IT or management team that it's approved — never enter client details, passwords, or confidential information into an unfamiliar AI platform.

featured.title: Most firms hit by AI security incidents, study finds
featured.quote: Most Australian organisations exposed to AI security breaches, requiring urgent defensive strategy.
